content.problemStatement:
Chef has a permutation PPP of length NNN consisting of integers 000 to N−1N-1N−1.

Help Chef answer QQQ queries of the following kind:


Given integers L1,L2,M1,L_1, L_2, M_1,L1​,L2​,M1​, and M2M_2M2​, compute the number of subsegments of PPP having length in the range [L1,L2][L_1, L_2][L1​,L2​] (both inclusive) and MEX in the range [M1,M2][M_1, M_2][M1​,M2​] (both inclusive).
Note:


The permutation PPP consists of all integers from 000 to N−1N-1N−1 exactly once.
A sequence of integers SSS is said to be a subsegment of PPP if it can be obtained by removing several (possibly zero) elements from the left of PPP and several (possibly zero) elements from the right of PPP. In particular, PPP is a subsegment of PPP while [][][] is **not** a subsegment of PPP.
The MEX of a sequence of integers is the smallest non-negative integer that does not belong to it.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the size of the permutation and the number of queries, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_{1}, P_{2},\ldots, P_{N}P1​,P2​,…,PN​, the permutation PPP.
Each of the next QQQ lines will contain a query denoted by four space separated integers L1,L2,M1,L_{1}, L_{2}, M_{1},L1​,L2​,M1​, and M2,M_{2},M2​, as defined in the statement.




Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ lines, where the ithi^{th}ith line denotes the answer to the ithi^{th}ith query.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
